subject: Re: Continuing frame processing after stopping
body:
Hey Josh,
WarpTools doesn’t have anything for on the fly processing (yet?) so this is expected behaviour - you can process subsets though if you know where it got cut off
Processing Subsets of Data - Warp, M and friends
warpem.github.io
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Jan 10, 2025, at 11:30, 'Joshua Hutchings' via Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:
﻿Hi all,
If I stop frame processing with WarpTools and then continue it, should it continue from where it left off when restarting it?
I'm processing a large dataset which was interrupted at ~80% of the way through. After repeating fs_motion_and_ctf, it detects the previously processed metadata, but still seems to re-process them anyway. 
Is this the expected behaviour, and is there a way to stop it reprocessing the processed frames? This is with WarpTools v2.0.0dev31.
Thanks!
Josh
